For consistency with the other service dispatchers (Radarr, Prowlarr, Overseerr, Notebook), this method should check if the Sonarr client is configured before attempting to use it. This prevents potential nil pointer dereferences if the service is not configured but a tool call is attempted.

```go
func (a *Agent) dispatchSonarr(ctx context.Context, name string, rawInput json.RawMessage) (string, bool, bool) {
	if a.sonarr == nil {
		switch name {
		case "search_series", "add_series", "get_queue", "get_history", "check_health", "remove_failed",
			"get_series_detail", "get_episodes", "get_logs", "manual_search", "get_quality_profiles",
			"get_blocklist", "get_root_folders", "get_download_clients", "update_series_monitoring",
			"trigger_series_search", "delete_series", "remove_blocklist_item", "grab_release":
			return jsonError("Sonarr integration is not configured"), true, true
		}
	}

fix: add nil guard for Sonarr client in dispatchSonarr

Add a nil check for the Sonarr client before dispatching tool calls,
consistent with the other service dispatchers (Radarr, Prowlarr,
Overseerr, Notebook). Prevents potential nil pointer dereference if
the service is not configured.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

content.text: Done. Added a nil guard for `a.sonarr` in `dispatchSonarr` at `internal/agent/dispatch_sonarr.go:12-19`, matching the pattern used by the Radarr, Prowlarr, Overseerr, and Notebook dispatchers. This prevents a nil pointer dereference if a Sonarr tool is called when the client isn't configured.

result: Done. Added a nil guard for `a.sonarr` in `dispatchSonarr` at `internal/agent/dispatch_sonarr.go:12-19`, matching the pattern used by the Radarr, Prowlarr, Overseerr, and Notebook dispatchers. This prevents a nil pointer dereference if a Sonarr tool is called when the client isn't configured.
